display.purpose: Hero headlines, primary identity moments. Use text-wrap: balance.

headline.purpose: Section openers, project detail H1, resume section heads.

title.purpose: Card titles, dialog headers, project card titles.

body.purpose: All paragraphs. Cap line length 65–75ch. text-wrap: pretty on long prose.

body-emphasis.displayName: Ember Emphasis (Fraunces italic in Working Heat)
body-emphasis.purpose: The em element — the system's most distinctive typographic gesture. Italic + ember + serif inline in sans body.

eyebrow.purpose: Section markers. ALWAYS paired with numbering (01 · Hero) to distinguish from AI-scaffolding eyebrows.

shadows.name: glass-inner-light
shadows.purpose: Top-edge highlight on the .glass primitive in light mode.

shadows.name: glass-outer-light
shadows.purpose: Diffuse outer drop on .glass in light mode.

shadows.name: glass-inner-dark
shadows.purpose: Top-edge highlight on .glass in dark mode (softer).

shadows.name: glass-outer-dark
shadows.purpose: Diffuse outer drop on .glass in dark mode (deeper, more diffuse).

shadows.name: bento-lift-light
shadows.purpose: Bento card hover state, light mode. Pairs with translateY(-2px) and accent-soft border.

shadows.name: card-lift-light
shadows.purpose: Glass .card hover state, light mode. Stronger than bento because glass competes with content behind.

shadows.name: nav-pill-light
shadows.purpose: Liquid Glass nav pill: inner soft glow + lifted drop. Ported from prototypes/liquid-glass-pill.

motion.name: ease-canonical
motion.purpose: The system's one ease. ease-out-quart equivalent. Used on card hovers, reveal-up scroll-reveals, mobile menu transitions. Held as --ease in :root.

motion.name: duration-hover
motion.purpose: Card and bento hover transitions (transform + box-shadow + border-color).

motion.name: duration-nav-bg
motion.purpose: Nav pill background/border/box-shadow transitions on theme swap and scroll-state changes.

motion.name: duration-mobile-menu
motion.purpose: Mobile menu panel transitions (transform + opacity + max-height).

motion.name: duration-hamburger-step
motion.purpose: Hamburger bar slide-then-rotate staged sequence (each leg is 150ms with 150ms delay between).

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.value: Crossfade-only, no translate; .reveal-up animation removed entirely.
motion.purpose: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) treatment. Already shipping in global.css for .reveal-up; extend the pattern to any new animation.

components.name: Bento Card (solid paper)
components.description: The canonical confident displayed-object container. Solid paper bg, hairline border, accent-soft hover border. No backdrop-filter — better paint cost on grids.
components.html:
organic-flow

Polish foundation events & passes

Astro on Cloudflare Workers, Supabase, Przelewy24, Apple & Google Wallet. I was the only engineer; the foundation is the customer.
components.css: .ds-bento { background: #faf8f3; color: #1a1a1c; border: 1px solid rgba(26, 26, 28, 0.12); border-radius: 20px; padding: 24px; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: transform 220ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-bento:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); border-color: oklch(64% 0.18 28 / 0.14); box-shadow: 0 16px 40px -16px rgba(26, 26, 28, 0.10); } .ds-bento-title { margin: 0 0 6px; font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.25rem; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: -0.005em; color: #1a1a1c; text-wrap: balance; } .ds-bento-meta { margin: 0 0 14px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; line-height: 1.2; color: #8e8a82; } .ds-bento-body { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.55; color: #1a1a1c; max-width: 65ch; } .ds-em { font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; color: oklch(64% 0.18 28); }

components.name: Glass Surface (translucent overlay)
components.description: Translucent panel with backdrop-filter blur + saturate. Used for the nav fallback, mobile side-wrap, pills. Different paint cost profile from .bento; reserve for surfaces that float over content.
components.html:
02 · About

A short note from the curator

Each artifact here runs. Click in.
components.css: .ds-glass { background: rgba(255, 252, 246, 0.45); color: #1a1a1c; -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(140%); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(140%); border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.55); border-radius: 14px; padding: 28px; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.55), 0 16px 40px -16px rgba(26, 26, 28, 0.18); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-glass-eyebrow { margin: 0 0 12px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.16em; color: #8e8a82; } .ds-glass-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.25rem; line-height: 1.3; color: #1a1a1c; text-wrap: balance; } .ds-glass-body { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.55; color: #4d4d52; }

components.name: Tech Pill (stack chip)
components.description: Skills & tools chip. Glass tint, mono caps. Inline, never a primary affordance.
components.html: Astro
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: rgba(255, 252, 246, 0.45); border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.55); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(140%); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(140%); border-radius: 999px; padding: 6px 12px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #4d4d52; line-height: 1.2; }

components.name: Editorial Eyebrow
components.description: Section marker. ALWAYS paired with numbering (01 · Hero). The numbering is what distinguishes this from AI-scaffolding eyebrows; do not ship the eyebrow without numbering or sequence logic.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { margin: 0 0 12px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.16em; color: #8e8a82; }

components.name: Accent Link
components.description: The system's link treatment for the ember-warm accent. Opacity-based hover, never an underline-on-hover.
components.css: .ds-link-accent { color: oklch(64% 0.18 28); text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: oklch(64% 0.18 28 / 0.4); text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-underline-offset: 3px; transition: color 0.2s ease, opacity 0.2s ease, text-decoration-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-link-accent:hover { opacity: 0.82; text-decoration-color: oklch(64% 0.18 28); }

components.name: Ember Italic Emphasis (em)
components.description: The system's most distinctive typographic gesture: italic Fraunces in Working Heat, inline in Geist body. Identity carried by inflection.
components.html: This site is a maker's cabinet — an organized exhibition where every artifact actually runs.
components.css: .ds-body { margin: 0; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.55; color: #1a1a1c; max-width: 65ch; } .ds-em { font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; color: oklch(64% 0.18 28); }

components.name: Section Eyebrow + Headline pair
components.description: The canonical section-opener pattern. Numbered eyebrow + Fraunces headline.
components.html:
03 · About

A solo developer who treats taste as a tool.
components.css: .ds-section-head { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0; max-width: 720px; } .ds-eyebrow { margin: 0 0 12px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.16em; color: #8e8a82; } .ds-headline { margin: 0; font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-weight: 400; font-size: clamp(1.75rem, 3.5vw, 2.5rem); line-height: 1.15; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #1a1a1c; text-wrap: balance; }

narrative.overview:
The site is a maker's cabinet: an organized exhibition of working artifacts, each one runnable, each one paid for in real engineering. The cabinet is lit by studio light, the type is ink on paper, the accent is the colour of a small ember at the edge of a workbench. Projects are displayed objects. The resume is the curator's notecard. The contact link is the door out. The Liquid Glass nav is the wandering eye that refracts whatever it passes over — it is the cabinet realizing it is also a screen.

The system commits to a warm-paper light surface (with a fully developed dark-mode counterpart), a deep ink ramp for type and structure, and a single ember-warm terracotta accent used sparingly. Typography pairs Fraunces with Geist and Geist Mono — three families on three contrast axes, never substituted for each other.

This system explicitly rejects two lanes: agency-portfolio-monochrome and generic-dev-portfolio. The committed warm-paper / terracotta / Fraunces / Liquid Glass identity is the antibody; drift toward neutral is the failure mode.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-paper light + ink dark with full token swap.
One ember-warm accent (Working Heat). Used sparingly on em, links, and hover states.
Three-family typography: Fraunces + Geist + Geist Mono. Self-hosted via @fontsource-variable.
Two surface registers: solid paper (.bento) for displayed objects; glass (.glass/.card/.pill) for overlays. A documented inconsistency lives between .card and .bento — consolidation recommended.
Motion is committed: one ease (cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)), uniform 220ms hover transitions, scroll-reveal gated to prefers-reduced-motion.
Liquid Glass nav is the signature: SVG feDisplacementMap chained into backdrop-filter with Safari/Firefox frosted-glass fallback.
A11y is engineered to the level the craft requires: real focus rings, reduced-motion respected, dark-mode complete. Not AAA-pursuing.

rules.name: The One Ember Rule
rules.body: Working Heat appears on ≤8% of any given surface. em is its canonical home — italic + ember + serif inline in sans body. Filling a button or a section bg with the accent breaks the metaphor.

rules.name: The Warm Paper Rule
rules.body: Light-mode bg is #f6f3ed. The committed warm tint is part of the brand and is deliberately NOT the AI-default warm-cream lane — it lives here as identity. New design moves do not retreat toward #ffffff.

rules.name: The Symmetric Dark-Mode Rule
rules.body: Every committed token has a dark-mode counterpart in the same role. New components must work in both modes from the first commit; 'dark mode pass later' is a deferred bug.

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Three families, three voices: Fraunces is the writer, Geist is the speaker, Geist Mono is the technician. They never substitute for each other. Headlines never go sans. Body never goes serif (except as em). Labels never go serif.

rules.name: The Ember-Italic Rule
rules.body: em is rendered as Fraunces italic in Working Heat. This pairing is the system's most distinctive typographic gesture; it carries identity inline with content. Do not override it. Do not use em purely decoratively.

rules.name: The Editorial Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: The section eyebrow uses Geist Mono uppercase tracked at 0.16em in Type Black Tertiary. It is paired with section numbering (01 · Hero) to signal sequence. Eyebrows without numbering, or eyebrows that decorate every block regardless of structure, are forbidden.

rules.name: The Flat-Paper Rule
rules.body: Most surfaces sit flat on the page. A bento card with a hairline border is the canonical container; cards lift only on hover, and the lift is short (220ms) and subtle. Heavy ambient shadows on rest-state surfaces are forbidden.

rules.name: The One Signature Rule
rules.body: Liquid Glass is the one signature elevation move in the system. New components do not add new signature treatments competing with it.

rules.name: The Symmetric Shadow Rule
rules.body: Every shadow has a dark-mode counterpart with darker, more diffuse parameters. Light-mode shadows use warm grey; dark-mode shadows use pure black at higher opacity.

dos: Do keep light-mode bg at #f6f3ed (Studio Light, warm paper). This is committed identity, not drift.
Do keep dark-mode parity: every committed token has a dark-mode counterpart and every new component works in both modes from the first commit.
Do use Fraunces italic + Working Heat on em for editorial emphasis. It is the system's most distinctive typographic gesture.
Do pair section eyebrows with section numbering (01 · Hero, 02 · Projects, etc).
Do use .bento for confident displayed objects.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion on every new animation.
Do keep the Liquid Glass module as the one signature elevation move.
Do include the dark-mode shadow pair on every new component that has a shadow.
donts: Don't drift the body bg toward pure white or pure off-white. The warm-paper tint is part of the brand.
Don't use Working Heat as a surface fill that dominates more than ~8% of a view.
Don't introduce a fourth type family.
Don't pair Fraunces with another serif.
Don't use em for purely decorative italics — it carries identity.
Don't ship an eyebrow without numbering or clear sequence logic.
Don't introduce a new card-shaped primitive; .card / .bento drift is already documented.
Don't apply background-clip: text with a gradient.
Don't scrap the Liquid Glass module's Safari/Firefox fallback.
Don't drift toward agency-portfolio-monochrome or generic-dev-portfolio.
Don't remove or invisibility-style focus rings.
